macro_segments.title: Historical Context: Rabban Gamliel's Conflict with Rabbi Yehoshua and the Separation of Authority in Different Lands

The Verse 'Eleh Moadei Hashem': Declaration Renders Holidays Binding Regardless of Astronomical Accuracy

micro_segments.title: The Fundamental Question: Is Beit Din Authority Limited to Kiddush HaChodesh or Extended to All Torah She-be'al Peh Interpretation?

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Dosa ben Horkinas' Reluctant Submission: The Principle That Beit Din Authority Is Binding Over Individuals (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Dosa's Statement: Auditing All Previous Beit Din Decisions Would Undermine Authority Entirely

Rabban Gamliel's Kiss and Dual Acknowledgment: Master in Wisdom, Student in Obedience

macro_segments.title: The Moon's Astronomical Cycle and the Limits of Authority: Rabban Gamliel Rejecting False Testimony Based on Science

micro_segments.title: The Story of Rabbi Chiya: Using Astronomy to Determine When Kiddush HaChodesh Can Occur
The Sky Filled with Clouds: A False Appearance of New Moon on Day 29, Rejected by Rabban Gamliel
Rabban Gamliel's Tradition: The Lunar Month Cannot Be Less Than 29 Days, 12 Hours, 40+ Minutes
The Precise Calculation: 29.5 Days Plus Two-Thirds of an Hour Plus 73 Chalakim (Within 0.2 Seconds)
The Chalakim System: Dividing the Hour Into 1080 Parts for Precision (18 Chalakim Per Minute)
Rabban Gamliel's Funeral Statement: Using Ben Zaza's Mother's Funeral to Publicly Negate False Kiddush

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Consolation: The Binding Power of Beit Din Decisions Through Midrashic Argument (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Play on 'Atem' (You): Three-fold Emphasis on Human Declaration as Binding Even in Error
Rabbi Yehoshua's Acceptance: 'You Have Consoled Me' (Nichamtani)

macro_segments.title: Broader Principles of Authority: The Judge in Your Generation vs. Earlier Generations (Yiftach, Bedan, Shimshon)

micro_segments.title: The Verse Vayomer Shmuel: Yiruba'al, Bedan, Yiftach, and Shmuel Are All Listed Together
Yiruba'al/Gideon, Bedan/Shimshon, Yiftach: Defining the Judges Referenced
The Midrash: Weighing Three 'Lightweights' Against Three 'Heavyweights' in the Same Category
The Teaching: In Your Generation, Your Authority Is As Great As Any Earlier Generation's
Even the Lightest of the Lightest, Once Appointed Leader Over Community, Becomes as the Mighty

macro_segments.title: The Verse's Instruction: Coming to the Judge of Your Day (El Ha-Shofet Asher Yihyeh Ba-Yamim Ha-Hem)

micro_segments.title: The Logical Question: How Can You Go to a Judge Not in Your Time? The Verse's Absurdity as Teaching Device
The Conclusion: You Must Go to the Judge in Your Generation, Period
Don't Bemoan Earlier Times: The Warning Against Nostalgia for Previous Generations
Rashi's Reading: You Have Only Yourselves to Blame for Not Having Better Leaders
Tosafot's Reading: Authority Is Not Based on Relative Greatness But on Office and Role

macro_segments.title: Rishonim and the Nature of Authority: Rambam on Legislative vs. Interpretive Powers of Beit Din

micro_segments.title: The Principle: A Later Beit Din Cannot Reverse an Earlier One Unless Greater in Wisdom and Number
Rambam's Distinction: Different Rules for Legislative vs. Interpretive Authority of Torah She-be'al Peh
The Practical Requirement: Only the Central Beit Din ha-Gadol Can Exercise This Reinterpretive Authority
The Implication: A Later Beit Din Could Theoretically Reinterpret Torah She-bi-Chtav Differently

macro_segments.title: The Reconciliation Scene: Rabban Gamliel's Public Recognition of Rabbi Yehoshua's Submission (continued)

micro_segments.title: The Significance of Public Submission: How Great Ones Listening to Small Ones Sets an Example

macro_segments.title: The Transition: Procedures When Beit Din Themselves See the Moon Versus Formal Witness Testimony

micro_segments.title: The Mishna's Three Scenarios: All Israel, Beit Din Alone, or Exactly Three Members Seeing the Moon
Case One: All Israel Saw It but Witnesses Not Formally Accepted Before Nightfall—Still a Pregnant Month
Case Two: Beit Din Alone Sees It; Two Break Off as Witnesses, Others Hear and Sanction
Case Three: Exactly Three Form Beit Din; Two Become Witnesses, Must Reconstitute Court with Two New Members
Principle: An Individual Cannot Empower Kiddush HaChodesh (Ein HaYachid Ne'eman Al Atzmo)

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Challenge to the Mishna: The Tension Between Actual Knowledge and Formal Witness Procedure

micro_segments.title: The Principle of Lo Tehei Smiah Gedolah Me-Re'iyah: Hearing Should Not Be Greater Than Seeing
Why Teach the Case When Everyone Saw It If You Need Formal Witnesses? The Gemara's Question
The Gemara's Answer: Even Public Knowledge Doesn't Override the Formal Declaration Requirement
Contrast with Dinei Mamonot: Unlike Financial Cases, Kiddush HaChodesh Cannot Span Day and Night
The Verse Ki Chok LeIsrael: The Declaration Must Be Made Before Day Ends, Like Other Judicial Acts
